min-brightness.title: Minimum Brightness
min-brightness.tooltip: Floor brightness applied in complete darkness. Must be at least 1% lower than Maximum Brightness.

max-brightness.title: Maximum Brightness
max-brightness.tooltip: Ceiling brightness applied at maximum ambient light. Must be at least 1% higher than Minimum Brightness.

show-label.title: Panel Display Mode
show-label.description: Choose how the applet appears in the panel. Show live status displays brightness percentage, icon-only shows just the icon, or hide to completely hide the applet from the panel.
show-label.tooltip: Controls the visibility and content of the panel applet.

label-display-mode.title: Panel Label Content
label-display-mode.description: Choose what the panel label shows while auto mode is running.
label-display-mode.tooltip: Only available when 'Show live status' is selected in Panel Display Mode.

response-curve.title: Response Curve
response-curve.description: Mathematical curve for mapping lux to brightness. Choose based on your environment and preference.
response-curve.tooltip: Logarithmic (default): Best for natural human perception, more sensitive to changes in dim light. Linear: Simple 1:1 mapping for predictable control in consistent environments. Sigmoidal: Sharp transition zone for environments with distinct lighting states (e.g., indoor vs outdoor).

calibration-guide.title: Calibration Instructions
calibration-guide.description:
1. Dark calibration: Go to your dimmest environment (e.g., dark room at night).
2. Click 'Calibrate Dark Level' and wait 10 seconds.
3. Bright calibration: Go to your brightest environment (e.g., direct sunlight).
4. Click 'Calibrate Bright Level' and wait 10 seconds.

These bounds define the lux range used by Logarithmic, Linear, and Sigmoidal curves.

calibrate-min-lux.tooltip: Click when in your dimmest environment (starts a 10s high-speed reading)

min-lux.title: Dark Reference Lux
min-lux.description: Ambient light level at or below which minimum brightness (0% of range) is applied.
min-lux.tooltip: Used as the lux range floor. At this lux or lower, brightness = Minimum Brightness.

calibrate-max-lux.tooltip: Click when in your brightest environment (starts a 10s high-speed reading)

max-lux.title: Bright Reference Lux
max-lux.description: Ambient light level at or above which maximum brightness (100% of range) is applied.
max-lux.tooltip: Used as the lux range ceiling. At this lux or higher, brightness = Maximum Brightness.

preview-curve-mapping.tooltip: Show a live table of lux values needed to reach 0%, 20%, 40%, 60%, 80%, and 100% brightness under each curve mode (using current min/max lux settings).

sensor-path.title: Sensor Path Override
sensor-path.tooltip: Only use this if auto-detection fails. Find your sensor by running 'find /sys -name illuminance' in terminal. Common paths are in /sys/bus/iio/devices/.

poll-interval.title: Poll Interval
poll-interval.tooltip: How often the ambient light sensor is read. Lower values are more responsive but use slightly more CPU.

smoothing-factor.title: Smoothing Factor
smoothing-factor.description: Sensor response speed: 0=instant react, 0.95=slowest react (most noise-resistant)
smoothing-factor.tooltip: Controls how quickly screen brightness follows sensor changes. Higher = slower to react = fewer flickers from sensor noise. Lower = faster reaction to real light changes. Maximum is 0.95 — a value of 1.0 would freeze brightness permanently and is not allowed.

lux-change-threshold.title: Lux Stability Threshold
lux-change-threshold.description: Minimum lux change required before recalculating brightness. Suppresses sensor noise-driven fluctuation.
lux-change-threshold.tooltip: When the ambient lux reading changes by less than this value, brightness is not recalculated. Set to 0 to react to every sensor reading. Increase (e.g. 5-10) if brightness flickers in stable lighting conditions.

min-brightness-delta.title: Minimum Brightness Change
min-brightness-delta.description: Minimum brightness percentage change required before applying a new value. Prevents tiny, barely-noticeable adjustments.
min-brightness-delta.tooltip: Brightness changes smaller than this value will be silently ignored. Increase to 3-5% for less frequent, more noticeable changes. Decrease to 1% for fine-grained control.

manual-debounce-window.title: Manual Override Cooldown
manual-debounce-window.description: Seconds to wait before resuming adaptive brightness after a manual brightness change.
manual-debounce-window.tooltip: After you manually change brightness, the applet waits this long before taking over again. Prevents fighting with external brightness daemons and OS idle-dimming.

schedule-enabled.title: Enable Scheduled Off Period
schedule-enabled.description: Automatically disable the applet during a daily recurring time window.
schedule-enabled.tooltip: When enabled, the applet will turn itself off between the start and end times, as if you clicked the panel icon.

schedule-off-start.title: Off Period Start
schedule-off-start.description: Time of day to automatically disable the applet.
schedule-off-start.tooltip: The applet will turn off at this time every day.

schedule-off-end.title: Off Period End
schedule-off-end.description: Time of day to automatically re-enable the applet.
schedule-off-end.tooltip: The applet will turn back on at this time every day. Supports crossing midnight (e.g., 22:00 to 06:00).

log-level.title: Log Level
log-level.description: Controls verbosity of the log file. Debug logs every sensor read.
log-level.tooltip: OFF: Logging disabled. Debug: Logs every sensor read (verbose, use for short troubleshooting). Info: Logs calibration events and errors (recommended for monitoring). Error: Only logs failures (minimal output).

show-log-file.tooltip: Opens adaptive-brightness.log in the default text editor
